data.title: uniQure is offering 5,789,473 shares at $47.50 each, raising approximately $300 million in total.
data.description: uniQure N.V. announced the pricing of its underwritten public offering of 5,789,473 ordinary shares at $47.50 per share, along with pre-funded warrants for 526,316 ordinary shares. The offering is expected to yield gross proceeds of around $300 million for uniQure before deductions. The underwriters have a 30-day option to buy up to 947,368 additional shares at the same price. The offering is set to close around September 29, 2025, pending standard closing conditions.

data.title: FF to deliver FF 91 2.0 to Calvin Gong on Oct 8. Signed deposit agreement with Pinnacle Real Estate Group for FX Super One in June.
data.description: During the month of June, Faraday Future entered into a deposit agreement with Pinnacle Real Estate Group for the FX Super One, which is a top-of-the-line EAI-MPV. This agreement was signed twice in June.

data.title: Bitcoin rally at crossroads with upcoming $22B options expiry and key U.S. inflation report.
data.description: Bitcoin's recent surge is at a critical juncture with the expiration of $22 billion worth of options coinciding with the release of a significant U.S. inflation report. This convergence of events could have a substantial impact on the ongoing rally of the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Dogecoin falls 5%, trading volume up by 23%, losing streak extends to 20%, open interest in futures drops by 4.53%.
data.description: Dogecoin (DOGE) experienced a significant drop alongside the wider cryptocurrency market on Thursday due to investor cautiousness amid macroeconomic uncertainties. DOGE, the largest meme coin by market capitalization, plummeted over 5% in the past 24 hours, surpassing losses in Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH). Its trading volume surged by 23% during this period, indicating heightened selling activity. The decline marked DOGE's continued downward trend, with a nearly 20% decrease in value over the past week. Speculative interest in the coin decreased, with open interest in DOGE futures falling by 4.53%.

data.title: Yum China earns spot on Fortune's 2025 "Change the World" list for KFC's Food Bank Initiative.
data.description: Yum China Holdings, Inc. (YUMC) has recently been listed on Fortune's "Change the World" 2025 roster for its impactful social initiatives. The company, traded under tickers NYSE: YUMC and HKEX: 9987, has been acknowledged for integrating projects aimed at benefiting society into its core operations. This prestigious recognition highlights Yum China's commitment to driving positive change, joining other leading businesses making significant contributions to societal welfare.

data.title: Investors target UAE as key Belt and Road real estate hub; promises growth amid global uncertainties.
data.description: On September 25, 2025, in Beijing, a report highlighted by China Daily noted that countries participating in the Belt and Road Initiative, including the United Arab Emirates, have become attractive opportunities for investors in light of worldwide economic instability. Amid ongoing global uncertainties, these nations present promising prospects for both individual and institutional investors looking to capitalize on growth and stability. This trend signifies a significant shift in investment interest towards regions involved in the initiative, showcasing the growing importance of these markets in the face of turbulent economic conditions.

data.title: 12 expats in Shanghai joined "Ride Like a Local" event to explore the city's bike-friendly vibe on Sept 20.
data.description: On September 20th, 2025, a total of 12 expatriates, consisting of international experience ambassadors as well as cycling enthusiasts, participated in the "Ride Like a Local" event in Shanghai. The group embarked on a cycling tour through the city, aiming to delve into the local culture and experience the streets of Shanghai firsthand.

data.title: Titomic names Aude Vignelles as Asia-Pacific President. Key: Leader in cold spray tech.
data.description: Titomic Limited (ASX: TTT), a prominent player in Titomic Kinetic Fusion™, a cutting-edge cold spray additive manufacturing technology, has appointed Aude Vignelles as the President of the Asia-Pacific Region (APAC). The announcement was made from Melbourne, Australia on September 25, 2025. Vignelles will be leading operations in the APAC region, leveraging the company's expertise in this field. This strategic move signifies Titomic's commitment to expanding its presence in this rapidly growing market.
